questions.stem: スピーチプレゼンテーションにおいて、ジェスチャーを使いながら話すのは ______★______ ______ 印象を悪くすることもあるので注意が必要だ。
opts: 大きすぎるジェスチャーは
questions.expl: Answer (★ Position) Answer 1: あまりに Correct Order 2 → 1 → 4 → 3 効果的である反面 → あまりに → 大きすぎるジェスチャーは → かえって Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown ② 効果的である反面 — “while (it) is effective” (sets contrast) ① あまりに — “too / overly” (degree modifier for the next noun phrase) ④ 大きすぎるジェスチャーは — “gestures that are too big (as for them)” (topic) ③ かえって — “rather / on the contrary” (introduces an opposite result) Reconstructed Sentence スピーチプレゼンテーションにおいて、ジェスチャーを使いながら話すのは効果的である反面、あまりに大きすぎるジェスチャーは、かえって印象を悪くすることもあるので注意が必要だ。 → “In presentations, while using gestures is effective, overly large gestures can actually hurt your impression, so caution is needed.” Why This Order Works • 『効果的である反面』 sets up a contrast. • 『あまりに』 directly modifies 『大きすぎるジェスチャー』. • Topic 『〜は』 followed by 『かえって』 naturally leads to the unexpected negative result.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (あまりに). 정답 순서: 効果的である反面 → あまりに → 大きすぎるジェスチャーは → かえって → 印象を悪くする. 핵심: '~反面'으로 대비를 시작하고, あまりに가 大きすぎる를 수식, かえって(오히려)가 부정적 결과를 도입. 제스처의 효과와 역효과를 대비하는 구조.

questions.stem: 今や、書籍や衣料品だけでなく生鮮食品も、インターネット______★______ ______時代である。
opts: 自宅にいながらにして
questions.expl: Answer (★ Position) Answer 3: 環境があれば Correct Order 4 → 3 → 1 → 2 アクセスが可能な → 環境があれば → 自宅にいながらにして → 手軽に購入できる Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown ④ アクセスが可能な — “accessible” (modifies 環境) ③ 環境があれば — “if there is an environment (with access)” (condition) ① 自宅にいながらにして — “while staying at home” (manner) ② 手軽に購入できる — “can be easily purchased” (result) Reconstructed Sentence 今や、書籍や衣料品だけでなく生鮮食品も、インターネットアクセスが可能な環境があれば、自宅にいながらにして手軽に購入できる時代である。 → “These days, not only books and clothing but even fresh food can be easily bought from home, provided you have internet access.” Why This Order Works • The adjective clause 『アクセスが可能な』 must come directly before the noun 『環境』. • 『〜があれば』 sets the condition, followed by manner 『自宅にいながらにして』 and the main predicate 『購入できる』.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (環境があれば). 정답 순서: アクセスが可能な → 環境があれば → 自宅にいながらにして → 手軽に購入できる. 핵심: 'アクセスが可能な'가 環境를 수식, '~ば'로 조건, '~ながらにして(~한 채로)'가 방법, 마지막에 결과 동사. 인터넷 환경만 있으면 집에서 살 수 있다는 흐름.

questions.stem: よく似た昆虫の判別は大変難しいという。中には、かなり ______ ______★______ 場合もあるそうだ。
questions.expl: Answer (★ Position) Answer 4: 迷うほど Correct Order 3 → 2 → 4 → 1 経験を積んだ → 昆虫学者でさえも → 迷うほど → 判別が難しい Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown ③ 経験を積んだ — “experienced” (modifies 昆虫学者) ② 昆虫学者でさえも — “even entomologists” (emphatic subject) ④ 迷うほど — “to the point of hesitating/being unsure” (degree) ① 判別が難しい — “is hard to distinguish” (predicate before 場合) Reconstructed Sentence よく似た昆虫の判別は大変難しいという。中には、かなり経験を積んだ昆虫学者でさえも迷うほど判別が難しい場合もあるそうだ。 → “They say telling apart very similar insects is quite difficult. In some cases, it’s so hard that even seasoned entomologists hesitate.” Why This Order Works • The noun modifier 『経験を積んだ』 must attach to 『昆虫学者』. • 『迷うほど』 naturally precedes an adjective/verb of difficulty, here 『判別が難しい』, to express degree.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (迷うほど). 정답 순서: 経験を積んだ → 昆虫学者でさえも → 迷うほど → 判別が難しい. 핵심: '経験を積んだ'가 곤충학자 수식, 'でさえも(~조차도)'로 강조, '~ほど(~할 정도로)'로 정도 표현. 경험 많은 학자조차 헷갈릴 정도로 어렵다는 강조 구조.

questions.stem: 今年の ______ ★______ ______ 気持ちのほうが大きかった。
opts: 少しも不安がなかったといえば
新たなことに挑戦できてうれしいという
questions.expl: Answer (★ Position) Answer 3: 少しも不安がなかったといえば Correct Order 1 → 3 → 2 → 4 転職にあたり → 少しも不安がなかったといえば → うそになるが → 新たなことに挑戦できてうれしいという Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown ① 転職にあたり — “upon changing jobs” (topic/time setting) ③ 少しも不安がなかったといえば — “if I were to say I had no anxiety at all” (concessive frame) ② うそになるが — “that would be a lie, but” (contrasting concession) ④ 新たなことに挑戦できてうれしいという — “(the) feeling of being happy to take on something new” (noun-modifying clause for 気持ち) Reconstructed Sentence 今年の転職にあたり、少しも不安がなかったといえばうそになるが、新たなことに挑戦できてうれしいという気持ちのほうが大きかった。 → “When changing jobs this year, it would be a lie to say I had no anxiety at all, but the feeling of happiness at being able to take on something new was stronger.” Why This Order Works • The concessive pattern 『〜といえば…うそになるが』 forms a single unit. • The long modifier 『新たなことに挑戦できてうれしいという』 must directly precede 『気持ち』.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (少しも不安がなかったといえば). 정답 순서: 転職にあたり → 少しも不安がなかったといえば → うそになるが → 新たなことに挑戦できてうれしいという(気持ち). 핵심: '~といえばうそになるが'는 '~라고 하면 거짓말이지만'의 양보 표현. 불안이 없었다고는 못 하지만, 새로운 도전에 대한 기쁨이 더 컸다는 흐름.

questions.stem: 北山市は ______ ______★______ のどかなところだった。
opts: 人口10万人を超える都市となったが
北山駅周辺以外にはほとんど何もない
questions.expl: Answer (★ Position) Answer 4: 30年前までは Correct Order 3 → 1 → 4 → 2 今でこそ → 人口10万人を超える都市となったが → 30年前までは → 北山駅周辺以外にはほとんど何もない Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown ③ 今でこそ — “nowadays / though now” (sets up contrast with past) ① 人口10万人を超える都市となったが — “it has become a city of over 100,000 people, but” (contrast clause) ④ 30年前までは — “until 30 years ago” (time frame for past state) ② 北山駅周辺以外にはほとんど何もない — “there was almost nothing except around Kitayama Station” (relative clause describing the place) Reconstructed Sentence 北山市は、今でこそ人口10万人を超える都市となったが、30年前までは北山駅周辺以外にはほとんど何もないのどかなところだった。 → “Kitayama City may now have over 100,000 people, but up until 30 years ago it was a peaceful place with almost nothing beyond the area around Kitayama Station.” Why This Order Works • 『今でこそ〜が』 contrasts the present with the past frame 『30年前までは』. • The relative clause about the station directly modifies 『のどかなところ』 to describe the former state.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (30年前までは). 정답 순서: 今でこそ → 人口10万人を超える都市となったが → 30年前までは → 北山駅周辺以外にはほとんど何もない(のどかなところ). 핵심: '今でこそ~が, ~までは…'로 현재와 과거를 대비. 지금은 큰 도시지만 30년 전엔 한적했다는 시간 대조 구조.

questions.expl: Translation “I’m not a narcissist. It’s not that my writing is such a grand novel as to stand up to repeated readings; it’s just like parents thinking their own child is the cutest.” Fully spelled out, the meaning is like: 「復読に耐えるほどたいそうな小説であっても（いないのだ）。」 → “It’s not that it’s such a great novel that it stands up to repeated reading.” Why the others are wrong: 2, The nuance is “I guess it isn’t,” not “that’s not the reason.” But the author isn’t guessing about his own work; he’s explaining the reason he rereads it. With 「でも」 in 小説でもあるまい, it sounds like: “It’s probably not even a novel (of that level).” which almost questions whether his work counts as that kind of novel at all. That doesn’t link smoothly to “It’s just parental affection.” We want “It’s not because it’s great,” not “It’s probably not great anyway.” 3 小説なのであろうか 復読に耐えるほどたいそうな 小説なのであろうか。 Meaning: “I wonder if it’s such a great novel that stands up to repeated readings?” This is a real question / doubt (“I wonder if…”). But the next line isn’t answering that question; it’s just giving a reason (“It’s parental affection”). The flow “I wonder if it’s great… It’s parental affection” is unnatural. The tone becomes “maybe it is that good?”, which moves toward praising his work, not denying narcissism. It clashes with his modest stance. 4 小説なのではないか Sentence: 復読に耐えるほどたいそうな 小説なのではないか。 Meaning: “Isn’t it such a great novel that stands up to repeated readings?” This is basically self-affirming: “Come to think of it, it is a p
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (小説であってもだ). 'たいそうな小説であってもだ'로 이어져 '대단한 소설이라 해도 (그렇지 않다)'라는 부정의 흐름. 핵심: 자신의 작품이 거듭 읽을 만큼 대단한 소설이라서가 아니라 단순히 부모 마음 같은 애착 때문이라는 겸손한 어조. 오답: 2. でもあるまい는 추측 부정, 3. のであろうか는 의문(자기 작품에 대해 의문은 어색), 4. のではないか는 자기 긍정으로 나르시스트 부정과 모순.

opts: 褒めたり叱ったりする
褒めたり叱ったりしたほうがいい
褒めたり叱ったりするようだ
褒めたり叱ったりしても仕方ない
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 褒めたり叱ったりする is correct. It means "to praise or scold." Question Translation While reading, I end up admiring or being utterly amazed, and praise or scold myself. Explanation The correct answer, 褒めたり叱ったりする, fits the context of the passage where the author describes their self-critique process while revisiting their old works. The act of re-reading one's own work leads to self-reflection, which naturally includes praising oneself when satisfied and scolding oneself when disappointed. The grammatical structure here, 褒めたり叱ったりする, implies an ongoing action of alternating between praising and scolding. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: 褒めたり叱ったりしたほうがいい suggests a recommendation for how the author should behave, but the sentence describes what naturally happens during re-reading, not a recommendation. - Answer 3: 褒めたり叱ったりするようだ implies a speculation or assumption, which doesn't fit since the author is discussing their personal experience. - Answer 4: 褒めたり叱ったりしても仕方ない implies resignation, a sense of futility in praising or scolding, which contrasts with the genuine engagement and reflection the author experiences. Grammar/Vocabulary Note The structure 〜たり〜たりする is used to indicate alternating actions or conditions. It shows that the author experiences both admiration and disappointment when engaging with their past work, highlighting a dynamic process of self-evaluation.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (褒めたり叱ったりする). '~たり~たりする'는 반복·교대 동작. 자기 작품을 다시 읽으며 감탄하기도 하고, 어이없어하기도 하고, 칭찬하기도 꾸짖기도 한다는 자연스러운 자기 평가의 흐름. 오답: 2. ~ほうがいい(권유)는 자기 행위 묘사에 부적합, 3. ~ようだ(추측)는 자신에 대한 추측이라 어색, 4. ~しても仕方ない(체념)는 적극적 자기 성찰과 모순.

opts: 否定せずにいられなくなる
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 否定せずにいられなくなる is correct. It means "unable to help but deny." Question Translation This sentence translates to: "Without being able to help but deny, it seems that my life’s work is something like that." Explanation The phrase "否定せずにいられなくなる" effectively conveys an inability to refrain from denying something. In the context of the passage, the author is reflecting on their life’s work and how they may critically view their past writing, yet cannot help but acknowledge and respect the effort and intention behind it. The use of "せずにいられなくなる" expresses a strong compulsion or an irresistible feeling, which fits well with the author's internal conflict regarding self-criticism versus respecting their past efforts. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: 否定しがちになる means "tend to deny." This implies a less intense and more habitual inclination towards denial, which doesn't capture the depth of inner compulsion as effectively as the correct answer. - Answer 3: 否定できなくなる means "unable to deny." This suggests an absolute inability to deny, which doesn't correspond with the author's struggle and respect for their past work. - Answer 4: 否定するようになる means "begin to deny." It indicates a transition rather than a compulsive state, thus not aligning with the inner conflict described. Grammar/Vocabulary Note The phrase "～せずにいられない" is a common expression in Japanese indicating someone cannot help but do something, conveying a sense of strong compulsion. This understanding
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (否定せずにいられなくなる). '~ずにいられない'는 '~하지 않을 수 없다'는 강한 충동. 글을 쓸 당시의 자신을 '부정하지 않을 수 없게 된다'는 뜻이지만, 그럼에도 옛 자신을 없애지 못한다는 갈등 구조. 오답: 2. ~がちになる(경향)는 약함, 3. できなくなる(부정 불가)는 정반대 의미, 4. ~ようになる(변화)는 강제성 부족.

questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: とは is correct. It means "is," "defines," or "explains what something is." Question Translation An entire life’s work [44] is likely to be like that. Explanation The word とは functions as a particle that is used to define or explain something. In this sentence, it's used to describe or define the concept of a "life's work." The sentence "一生の仕事[44]そうしたものであろうと思う" implies that the speaker believes their life's work to be something that parallels the described acts of respect for past works and the responsibility to future writing. Thus, linking とは to this sentiment makes clear the definition or essence of what constitutes their life's work. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: も含めて（もふくめて）means "including" or "together with," which doesn't fit the context as the sentence is about defining or explaining what a life’s work is, not listing or including items. - Answer 2: でない限り（でないかぎり）means "unless" or "as long as it is not," which is conditional and doesn't correctly explain or define what is meant by the "life's work." - Answer 3: にしても（にしても）means "even if" or "even so," which implies concession or contrast, not a direct definition or explanation of "life's work." Grammar/Vocabulary Note The usage of とは is often seen in sentences where the speaker is defining or explaining a phrase or idea, providing a succinct definition. It is an essential term for articulating concepts, especially in written Japanese where defining abstract ideas is crucial.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (とは). 'とは'는 정의·설명을 도입하는 조사. '一生の仕事とはそうしたものであろう(평생의 일이란 그러한 것일 것이다)'로 평생의 일에 대한 정의를 내림. 오답: 1. も含めて(포함), 2. でない限り(조건), 3. にしても(양보)는 모두 정의·설명에 부적합. 추상 개념을 정의할 때 핵심 패턴.

questions.stem: 筆者の考えに合うのはどれか。
opts: 情報を求める人ほど、知らない情報があることに不安を感じる。
情報を得ることに熱心な人は、世間で流行っていることしか知らない。
多くの情報から必要なものが選べなければ、世間から遅れてしまう。
流行っているものの情報に熱心なだけでは、情報化社会で生きられない。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 情報を求める人ほど、知らない情報があることに不安を感じる。 This answer is correct. It means "The more people seek information, the more anxious they feel about not knowing some information." Question Translation Which opinion aligns with the author's view? Passage: "We can call it an information society or an information-oriented society, but when trying to live earnestly in it, there are cries for help that emerge. Namely, there is too much information and people don't know which is true or what should be chosen. The more diligently someone pays attention to trending information, the more they're driven by the anxiety of not knowing what's trending in society and feeling like they're falling behind, which is a characteristic of an information-oriented society." Explanation The correct answer captures the main point conveyed in the passage—that in an information society, the abundance of information causes people who seek it to feel anxiety about not knowing certain things. The passage emphasizes that this is a characteristic of people trying to live in an information society—the sheer volume of information and the resulting FOMO (fear of missing out) cause unease. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: "People who are enthusiastic about obtaining information only know what's trending in society." This incorrectly suggests a limitation on knowledge to only trends, whereas the passage speaks about the anxiety regarding missing information, not about narrow knowledge. - Answer
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (정보를 추구하는 사람일수록 모르는 정보가 있는 것에 불안을 느낀다). 본문은 정보화사회에서 사람들이 너무 많은 정보 속에서 무엇이 진실이고 무엇을 골라야 할지 모르며, 유행을 모르면 뒤처질까 불안해한다고 설명. 오답: 2. '유행하는 것밖에 모른다'는 본문의 지식 한정 주장 아님, 3. 본문은 사람들의 초조감을 말할 뿐 결과를 단정하지 않음, 4. 본문 주장과 어긋남.

questions.stem: 筆者によると、「書くこと」によってどうなるか。
opts: 自身の新しい考え方に気づく。
自身の意見を具体的に伝えられる。
自身の意見を批判的に見ることができる。
自身のこころの変化を観察することができる。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: 自身の意見を批判的に見ることができる is correct. It means "to be able to view one's own opinions critically." Question Translation According to the author, what happens through the act of "writing"? Explanation The passage explains the transformation that occurs when abstract thoughts ("思ったこと" and "考えたこと") are written down and become concrete. This process allows the writer to directly see and objectively observe their own thoughts. The passage states that observation ("観察") almost inevitably leads to a critical mindset ("批判のこころ"), highlighting the realization of weaknesses or immaturity in one's thoughts at the moment they are written. Thus, writing allows for critical self-reflection of one’s own opinions or ideas, making answer 3 the best fit. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 自身の新しい考え方に気づく ("to notice one's new way of thinking") is incorrect because the passage focuses on the critical observation of current thoughts, not the emergence or realization of new thoughts. - Answer 2: 自身の意見を具体的に伝えられる ("to be able to convey one's opinion concretely") is incorrect because the focus is not on communicating thoughts to others but on internally observing and critiquing them. - Answer 4: 自身のこころの変化を観察することができる ("to be able to observe changes in one’s own mind") is incorrect because the passage focuses on critical observation of the written thoughts at the moment of writing rather than observing changes over time. Grammar/Vocabulary Note The term "批判" (ひはん) refers to "cr
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (자신의 의견을 비판적으로 볼 수 있다). 본문은 '쓰는 것'이 추상적 사고를 구체화해 객관적 관찰로 이어지고, 관찰이 비판의 마음을 불러일으킨다고 설명. 오답: 1. 새로운 사고방식 발견은 주제가 아님, 2. 타인에게 전달하는 것이 아닌 자기 관찰이 핵심, 4. '시간에 따른 마음의 변화'가 아니라 '쓰는 순간의 비판적 자기 인식'.

questions.stem: 筆者の考えに合うのはどれか。
opts: 消費者心理を捉えた広告作りは、昔より難しくなっている。
消費者心理を捉えていないのは、良い広告とは言えない。
消費者心理を捉えていれば、広告は表現にこだわる必要はない。
消費者心理を捉えているだけでは、効き目のある広告にはならない。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 2: 消費者心理を捉えていないのは、良い広告とは言えない。 It means, "Advertisements that do not capture consumer psychology cannot be called good." Question Translation The author agrees with which of the following? Passage: (3) Compared to the past, it seems that products are not selling as well, and the effectiveness of advertisements is also declining. As a result, there is a tendency to focus only on the novelty and creativity of the expression. In short, advertisements that should be primarily focused on the crucial consumer psychology are failing to fully address it or analyze it, resulting in more superficial, expression-focused ads. While consumer psychology can be understood as the "essence," and expression as the "method of conveyance," how can it be appropriate if the most important essence is ignored, and only superficial methods of conveyance are emphasized? Without essence, a good method of conveyance cannot exist. Explanation The author's main argument is that advertisements are becoming more focused on the superficial aspects (the method of conveyance) rather than addressing the fundamental consumer psychology (the essence). In the passage, the author emphasizes the importance of capturing consumer psychology for an advertisement to be effective. Hence, the correct answer states that "Advertisements that do not capture consumer psychology cannot be called good," aligning directly with the author's viewpoint. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 消費者心理を捉えた広告作りは
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (소비자 심리를 잡지 못하면 좋은 광고라고 할 수 없다). 필자는 본질(소비자 심리)을 무시하고 표면적 표현에만 치중하는 광고가 늘고 있다며, '본질 없이 좋은 전달 방식은 존재하지 않는다'고 주장. 오답: 1. 어려워졌다는 비교는 주장 아님, 3. 표현이 필요 없다는 주장도 아님, 4. 본문은 본질이 있어야 한다는 입장이지 본질만으론 부족하다는 입장이 아님.

questions.stem: 対話について、筆者はどのように述べているか。
opts: 他人とじぶんの違いが大きいほど、熱い対話ができる。
他人との思考の違いを把握することで、自身の思考が深められる。
わかりあえないという戸惑いや痛みが克服でき、他人と共感できる。
思いや考えを表現する能力が鍛えられ、他人に理解してもらいやすくなる。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 2: 他人との思考の違いを把握することで、自身の思考が深められる。 (By understanding the differences in thoughts with others, one's own thinking deepens.) Question Translation What does the author say about dialogue? Explanation Answer 2 is correct because the passage discusses how engaging in dialogue helps understand differences between oneself and others on a more subtle level. This process helps deepen one's own thinking as it involves careful consideration of others' different thoughts and opinions and using that reflection to re-evaluate one's own ideas. This harmonizes well with the statement in Answer 2 about deepening one's thinking by recognizing thought differences. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 他人とじぶんの違いが大きいほど、熱い対話ができる。 (The greater the difference between oneself and others, the more intense the dialogue can be.) This is incorrect because the passage indicates that more intense dialogue comes not from merely having large differences but from starting with discomfort and being open to essential differences. - Answer 3: わかりあえないという戸惑いや痛みが克服でき、他人と共感できる。 (Being able to overcome the confusion or pain of not understanding each other leads to empathy with others.) This is incorrect because the text does not state overcoming confusion or pain leads to empathy. Rather, it discusses beginning with and being open to those feelings to improve dialogue depth. - Answer 4: 思いや考えを表現する能力が鍛えられ、他人に理解してもらいやすくなる。 (The ability to express thoughts and ideas is honed, making it easier

questions.stem: 筆者によると、学問とはどのような行為か。
opts: 「正解」を求めて、自ら主体的に問い続けていく行為である。
独創的なアイデアを生み出すために、自らのものの見方を変える行為だ。
自ら問いながら、ものの見方を模索しながら自分自身が変わっていく行為だ。
新しい知識を積極的に取り入れることで、自分自身を変えていく行為だ。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: 自ら問いながら、ものの見方を模索しながら自分自身が変わっていく行為だ。 This means "The act of questioning oneself while exploring new perspectives, leading to personal change." Question Translation According to the author, what kind of activity is academic inquiry? Explanation The passage describes academic inquiry as an active process where one questions conventional viewpoints and seeks new perspectives. It emphasizes the transformation of oneself through this process, rather than merely seeking a single "correct answer." The passage stresses that learning is not just about accumulating knowledge or remembering information for tests, but about reorganizing it to make it one's own, which results in personal change. Therefore, Answer 3, which aligns with these themes of personal transformation and exploration through questioning, is the best fit. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 「正解」を求めて、自ら主体的に問い続けていく行為である。 This option focuses on seeking the "correct answer," which the passage specifically states is not the essence of academic inquiry, as it is not always about finding a single answer. - Answer 2: 独創的なアイデアを生み出すために、自らのものの見方を変える行為だ。 While this option mentions changing one's perspectives to create original ideas, it doesn't encompass the broader aspect of personal change and continuous questioning described in the passage. - Answer 4: 新しい知識を積極的に取り入れることで、自分自身を変えていく行為だ。 This focuses on acquiring new knowledge, which is not the primary focus of the passage. The passage describes l
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (스스로 묻고 사물을 보는 방식을 모색하면서 자신이 변해 가는 행위). 본문은 학문이 능동적 질문을 통해 상식을 의심하고, 지식의 재편성과 자기 변화를 가져오는 행위라 정의. 오답: 1. '정답'을 추구한다는 부분이 본문과 모순(정답은 하나가 아니라고 함), 2. 독창적 아이디어는 결과일 뿐, 4. 단순한 지식 흡수가 아니라 재편성·자기 변화가 핵심.

questions.stem: 学問で「疑う」ことについて、筆者の考えに合うのはどれか。
opts: 何を信じればいいのかを理解するのに欠かせない行為だ。
豊かに生きるために、新しい常識を作りだす積極的な行為だ。

questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: 常識に縛られずに、これまでと異なる考え方を生み出す行為だ is correct. It means "an act of creating a different way of thinking not bound by common sense." Question Translation What aligns with the author's view on the concept of "questioning" in academics? Explanation The passage discusses the importance of "doubting" or questioning in an academic context. The author suggests that questioning common sense can lead to the discovery of new perspectives, emphasizing that academic learning involves rethinking and transforming one's worldview rather than rote memorization. This aligns with Answer 4, as it highlights the creation of new ways of thinking that are unconstrained by conventional norms. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: "何を信じればいいのかを理解するのに欠かせない行為" translates to "an essential act in understanding what to believe." The text does not emphasize understanding belief, but rather the creation of new ideas and perspectives. - Answer 2: "豊かに生きるために、新しい常識を作りだす積極的な行為" translates to "an active act of creating new norms for living richly." While the text supports proactive questioning, it doesn't state that the aim is to form new common sense, but rather to explore creative and varied perspectives. - Answer 3: "常識に安易に取り込まれないために必要だが、否定的な行為" translates to "necessary to avoid being easily absorbed by common sense but a negative act." The passage refutes the idea that doubting is negative; it is portrayed as a positive, creative process. Grammar/Vocabulary Note The key concept here

questions.stem: 筆者によると、アリはなぜカタクリの種を巣に運ぶのか。
opts: 種についている脂肪分がアリの好物だから。
種はアリの成長に欠かせない栄養分だから。
種がアリの餌になる幼虫に似ているから。
種をアリの生きた幼虫だと思うから。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: 種をアリの生きた幼虫だと思うから is correct. It means "because the ants think the seeds are their living larvae." Question Translation According to the author, why do ants carry the seeds of the katakuri plant to their nest? Explanation The passage explains that the seeds of the katakuri plant have a fatty part known as an elaiosome. This substance has a smell similar to that of ant larvae. Therefore, the ants mistakenly think the seeds are their larvae and carry them to their nests. Answer 4 correctly captures that ants take the seeds to their nest because they think the seeds are living larvae. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: Although the elaiosome is attractive due to its fatty composition, the passage specifies that the ants are more attracted because they associate it with their larvae, not just because it's a favored food. - Answer 2: The passage doesn't mention the seeds providing essential nutrients for ant growth. The primary reason given is the mistaken identity related to the smell. - Answer 3: While the elaiosome's smell is similar to the ants' larvae, it doesn't say that the seeds resemble larvae in form to become the ants' food. The passage clearly distinguishes that ants are not collecting seeds for food but because of the mistaken identity with their larvae. Grammar/Vocabulary Note 惹かれる (ひかれる) - This term means "to be attracted or drawn to" and is central to understanding why ants are interested in elaiosome. Recognizing how sensory cues s
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (씨를 개미의 살아 있는 유충이라고 생각하기 때문). 엘라이오솜이 개미 유충 냄새와 비슷해 개미가 자기 유충으로 착각해 둥지로 옮긴다는 본문 내용. 오답: 1. 지방 좋아하기 때문이 아니라 유충 냄새 때문, 2. 영양 공급 언급 없음, 3. '먹이 유충과 비슷'이 아니라 '자기 유충으로 오인'이 핵심.

questions.stem: 筆者によると、アリはなぜ集めた種を捨てるのか。
opts: 種のにおいで、アリの幼虫が死んでしまうから。
種からアリの死んだ幼虫のにおいがするから。
種がアリの 死んだ幼虫と同じ色に変わるから。
種がはじけて、アリの嫌うにおいを出すから。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 2: 種からアリの死んだ幼虫のにおいがするから. It means "Because the seeds emit a smell similar to that of dead ant larvae." Question Translation According to the author, why do ants discard the seeds they collect? Explanation The passage describes a fascinating strategy of the plant called Katakuri, which involves ants in its seed dispersal. Initially, ants are attracted to the seeds because of a substance called elaiosome, which emits a smell similar to that of their larvae. However, after about 24 hours, the seeds start to emit a smell akin to dead ant larvae. This change in smell prompts ants to discard the seeds, thus assisting in the plant's seed dispersal process. Option 2 correctly reflects this shift in odor from the seeds being akin to dead ant larvae, explaining why ants remove them from their nests. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: This choice suggests the seeds' smell causes ant larvae to die, which is incorrect. The passage states that the smell changes to resemble that of dead larvae, not that it harms them. - Answer 3: This option provides an incorrect reasoning that the color of the seeds changes to that of dead ant larvae, which is not mentioned in the passage. The text only refers to a change in smell. - Answer 4: Suggests that the seeds emit a smell disliked by ants once they burst, which is a misinterpretation. The seeds release a specific smell resembling that of dead larvae, not simply an unpleasant odor to ants. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - 惹かれ
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (씨에서 죽은 개미 유충의 냄새가 나기 때문). 본문은 24시간 후 엘라이오솜의 냄새가 죽은 유충 냄새로 변해 개미가 둥지 밖으로 버린다고 설명. 오답: 1. 냄새가 유충을 죽이는 게 아님, 3. 색깔 변화 언급 없음(냄새만 변함), 4. 단순히 싫은 냄새가 아니라 '죽은 유충 냄새'가 정확한 묘사.

questions.stem: 「そんな言い方をするもんじゃない」と言ったら負けだとあるが、なぜか。
opts: こどもの成長を否定しているようなものだから。
こどもの反論が正しいと認めるようなものだから。
自分で自分を注意しているようなものだから。
自分がこどもと同じレベルで答えるようなものだから。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: 自分で自分を注意しているようなものだから。 It means "It's like you're scolding yourself." Question Translation The question: "'タとあるが、なぜか。" translates to "It says, 'It's like you're scolding yourself.' Why is that?" Explanation In the passage, it is suggested that when parents tell their children, "そんな言い方をするもんじゃない" (You shouldn't speak like that), they might end up being chided themselves with a remark like "That sounds exactly like you." This indicates that the child's behavior is a reflection of the parent's own behavior, effectively making it as if the parent is indirectly criticizing themselves for the way they speak. This is why Answer 3 is correct: if the child mirrors the parent's behavior and the parent scolds the child for it, they are essentially admonishing their own behavior. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: こどもの成長を否定しているようなものだから (It's like denying the child's growth): The passage doesn't discuss denying a child's growth; it focuses on how children imitate their parents. - Answer 2: こどもの反論が正しいと認めるようなものだから (It's like admitting the child's argument is correct): The emphasis isn't on the correctness of the child's argument but on imitation of the parent's mannerisms. - Answer 4: 自分がこどもと同じレベルで答えるようなものだから (It's like responding at the child's level): The issue is not about responding at the same level but the self-reflection in the child's imitation. Grammar/Vocabulary Note A key concept in the passage is "物真似" (ものまね, mimicry), which is the act of imitatio

questions.stem: こどもの模倣について、筆者はどのように考えているか。
opts: 完璧にはコピーできないことが、個性につながっている。
完璧にコピーしようとしているが、本質は捉えられていない。
オリジナルなものを自由に変えて、おとなを楽しませようとしている。
オリジナルからの逸脱を気にせず、自分の個性を表現しようとしている。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 完璧にはコピーできないことが、個性につながっている。 It means "The inability to perfectly copy leads to individuality." Question Translation What does the author think about children's imitation? Explanation The passage discusses how children are known for mimicking others and highlights the uniqueness that comes from their imitation. While children aim to copy adults or things they observe, they can't do so perfectly because of their developing abilities. As a result, they end up creating something unique and original through their deviations. This act of imperfect copying is what fosters their individuality. Hence, Answer 1 is correct because it encapsulates the main idea that the passage conveys about children's imitation related to their individuality. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: 完璧にコピーしようとしているが、本質は捉えられていない。 This suggests children try to copy perfectly but miss the essence, which is not supported by the passage. The passage emphasizes that children do not achieve perfect copies due to their developmental limitations, which leads to individuality, not a failure to capture the essence. - Answer 3: オリジナルなものを自由に変えて、おとなを楽しませようとしている。 This implies children purposefully modify originals to entertain adults. The text does not suggest an intent to entertain but highlights the natural process of deviation that results in something new and unique. - Answer 4: オリジナルからの逸脱を気にせず、自分の個性を表現しようとしている。 This suggests children consciously express their individuality without caring

questions.stem: 人間が使用するものを作る際の問題点について、筆者が述べていることはどれか。
opts: 同じもので多様な使い手に対応すると、そのものの効果に限界が生じる。
同じものを多様な使い手に対応させるには、開発に時間がかかりすぎる。

questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1 is correct. It means, "When using the same product to cater to diverse users, the effectiveness of that product becomes limited." Question Translation What does the author mention as the problem concerning making things that humans use? Explanation In the passage, the author discusses two approaches to designing products for people with diverse body types and capabilities. The first approach, which is economically advantageous, involves using the same product for diverse users. However, it often leads to limitations in effectiveness, as explicitly noted in the passage. Thus, Answer 1 accurately reflects the author's point regarding this approach. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: It states that developing the same product to cater to diverse users takes too much time. This is incorrect because the passage mentions time constraints only in the context of the second approach, not the first. - Answer 3: This option suggests that addressing individual needs makes the product's effectiveness less visible. However, the passage does not mention visibility of effectiveness as an issue with catering to individual users. - Answer 4: This option states that catering to individual users makes it so that products can't be used equally. The passage actually suggests that catering to individual needs makes it more challenging due to development time and cost, not equality issues. questions.stem: ロボピーが、ほかのロボットと異なる点は何か。

questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: どう動くかというプログラムを多数組み込んだので、動きが予測できない。 It means that many programs determining how it moves have been incorporated, making its movements unpredictable. Question Translation What is the point that makes Robo-P different from other robots? Explanation The passage describes how Robo-P is programmed with over 700 rules and 300 program actions, which lead to complex and diverse movements. As a result, even the developers can't predict how it will react or move at any given time. This unpredictability in movement is central to the passage and makes Answer 3 the correct choice because it highlights that the extensive programming results in an unpredictable sequence of actions. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 動作パターンが限られているので、ある程度動きが予測できる。 This option claims that the patterns are limited, allowing for predictable movement, which contradicts the passage's emphasis on unpredictability and the elaborate programming. - Answer 2: 与えられた情報の一部を自分で判断して動くので、動きが予測できない。 While it mentions unpredictability, it incorrectly implies that Robo-P independently judges information, whereas the passage indicates that all movements are pre-programmed but complexly interconnected, resulting in unpredictable output. - Answer 4: 多様で複雑な動作をプログラムしたので、人間のように自然な動きができる。 This answer suggests that the programming results in human-like natural movements, which is not mentioned in the text and strays from the concept of unpredictability discussed. Grammar/Vocabulary Note - 動作パターン (どうさパター
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (어떻게 움직일지에 대한 프로그램을 다수 짜 넣었기 때문에 움직임을 예측할 수 없다). 700개 이상의 규칙과 300개 이상의 동작을 조합해 개발자조차 예측 불가능하게 만들었다는 점이 차이. 오답: 1. 한정된 패턴이 아니므로 정반대, 2. 자기 판단이 아니라 프로그램 작동, 4. 인간 같은 자연스러운 움직임 언급 없음.

questions.stem: どこかへ向かって歩きだしたとあるが、 その時筆者にはどのように思えたか。
opts: プログラムに間違いがあり、ロボピーに意思が生まれて動いた。
プログラムが作動したのではなく、ロボピー自身が考えて動いた。
プログラムが作動して、意思があるかのようにロボピーが動いた。
プログラムが誤作動して、制作者の意図に反してロボピーが動いた。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: プログラムが作動して、意思があるかのようにロボピーが動いた。 This means "The program activated and Robopee moved as if it had its own intention." Question Translation The sentence asks, "What did the writer think when it seemed like the robot started moving towards somewhere during their meeting?" Explanation In the passage, the writer describes how the robot, Robopee, was programmed with numerous complex behaviors, making its actions unpredictable. During a meeting, Robopee recognized their voices, moved around, and spoke as if it had its own intentions, although it was just following a programmed sequence. The passage emphasizes that it seemed like Robopee had its own "will," though it was actually a predetermined response. Answer 3 accurately reflects the situation—the program was operating normally, conveying an impression of intention, which aligns with the writer's observations. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: Suggests an error in the program causing Robopee to develop intention. However, the passage states it was functioning as programmed with no mention of errors. - Answer 2: Implies that Robopee acted independently, but it was indeed the program’s normal operation that made it seem intentional. - Answer 4: Implies a malfunction contrary to the creator's intent. This is incorrect since the behavior was part of the programmed complexity. Grammar/Vocabulary Note Understanding "意思" (いし, will/intention) is key. questions.stem: 「心」について、筆者はどのように考えているか。
opts: あると思って観察すれば、複雑な動きの意図が理解できる。
実体がないので、あるとかないとか想像しても意味がない。
仕組みが複雑すぎるため、人間同士であっても想像できない。
理解しきれない複雑な動きを見たときに、人間が想像するものだ。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: 理解しきれない複雑な動きを見たときに、人間が想像するものだ. This is correct. It means "it is something that humans imagine when they see complex movements that they cannot fully understand." Question Translation What does the author think about "heart/mind"? Explanation The passage describes a scenario where the researchers felt the robot exhibited a personality or decision-making ability, although they recognized it was merely executing a programmed response. The author concludes that a "heart" or "mind" is not something intrinsic to an object or being; rather, it exists in the perception and imagination of the observer when they encounter something complex beyond their complete understanding. Thus, the correct answer reflects the idea that the concept of "heart" or "mind" arises from human imagination in response to complex and inscrutable phenomena. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: "あると思って観察すれば、複雑な動きの意図が理解できる。" This implies that by observing with the belief of its existence, one can understand the intentions behind complex movements. This contradicts the author's point, which stresses imagination due to complexity, not understanding. - Answer 2: "実体がないので、あるとかないとか想像しても意味がない。" This suggests it's meaningless to imagine its existence or non-existence because it lacks substance. The passage conveys that although "heart" might not be a tangible reality, the imagination and perceived existence are meaningful to the viewer. - Answer 3: "仕組みが複雑すぎるため、人間同士であっても想像できない。" This in
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (이해할 수 없는 복잡한 움직임을 보았을 때 인간이 상상하는 것). 필자는 마음이 복잡한 대상에 실재하기보다, 그 움직임을 보는 사람이 상상하는 것이라고 결론. 오답: 1. 관찰로 의도를 이해한다는 주장이 아니라 상상이 핵심, 2. 의미 없다는 결론 아님, 3. 사람끼리는 서로 마음이 있다고 느낀다고 했으므로 '상상 불가'와 모순.

questions.stem: メモを取ることについて、 A と B は同のように述べているか。
opts: A も B も、量よりも書きとる内容を意識して取ることが重要だと述べている。
A も B も、できるだけ多くの情報を正確に書き取ったほうがいいと述べている。
A は内容を活用するために必要だと述べ、 B は取ることに集中し過ぎては意味がないと述べている。
A は問題点や感想なども一緒に記入したほうがいいと述べ、 B は話の内容を理解していなければ正確には取れないと述べている。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: A は内容を活用するために必要だと述べ、 B は取ることに集中し過ぎては意味がないと述べている。 This means "A states that taking notes is necessary to utilize the content, while B states that overly focusing on taking notes makes it meaningless." Question Translation Regarding taking notes, how do individuals A and B express themselves similarly? Explanation In the passage, person A talks about taking notes to absorb information and use it in the future, emphasizing the value of making ideas visible and conscious through note-taking. A believes notes help maximize the absorption of ideas. Conversely, person B points out the drawbacks of focusing too much on taking notes without understanding the information. B advises engaging with the content and only using notes as a guideline for organizing thoughts later. Thus, A emphasizes utility, whereas B cautions against the excess. This contrast makes Answer 3 the best fit. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: States that both A and B discuss the importance of what content to note rather than the quantity. However, A's focus is on using notes for future reference, while B emphasizes that taking notes shouldn't detract from comprehension. - Answer 2: Attributes a preference for accuracy and quantity of information in notes to both A and B. This misrepresents their views. questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (A는 내용 활용을 위해 필요하다고, B는 메모에만 집중하면 의미가 없다고 서술). A는 메모로 정보를 흡수해 활용하는 가치를 강조하고, B는 메모에 매몰되어 내용 이해를 놓치는 것을 경고. 오답: 1. A·B 양쪽 모두 '내용 의식'이 핵심이라 단언 못 함, 2. 양적·정확성 강조는 B의 비판 대상, 4. 구체 항목 묘사가 본문과 다름.

questions.stem: 話の聞き方について、 A と B の認識で共通していることは何か。
opts: 何かを得ようという姿勢で聞くことが重要だ。
その場ですべてを理解しようとしなくてもいい。
手を動かしながら聞いたほうが、記憶に残りやすい。
興味や関心を示し、話し手に安心感を与えることが大切だ。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 1: 何かを得ようという姿勢で聞くことが重要だ。 It means "It is important to listen with the attitude of trying to gain something." Question Translation What do A and B have in common regarding their understanding of listening to a talk? Explanation Both A and B emphasize the importance of listening with the intent to gain something. A discusses the idea of being conscious of capturing new insights and ideas, suggesting that one should actively seek out learning opportunities from everyday conversations. Similarly, B emphasizes a proactive listening approach where the focus is on understanding and relating the information to oneself, rather than just passively recording it. Hence, the answer that best captures this shared perspective is about the significance of listening with the purpose of gaining something. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 2: "その場ですべてを理解しようとしなくてもいい。" (It's okay not to understand everything on the spot.) - This perspective is not explicitly shared between A and B. A does not suggest it’s okay not to understand everything immediately, as both stress active involvement in the listening process. - Answer 3: "手を動かしながら聞いたほうが、記憶に残りやすい。" (Listening while writing helps retain information better.) - This idea is specific to A's perspective, emphasizing the role of taking notes to reinforce memory. B critiques this as it might interfere with understanding the content. - Answer 4: "興味や関心を示し、話し手に安心感を与えることが大切だ。" (It is important to show interest and give the spea
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번 (무언가를 얻으려는 자세로 듣는 것이 중요하다). A는 적극적 흡수와 발견을, B는 자신과 관련지어 주체적으로 듣기를 강조하므로 '얻으려는 자세'가 공통점. 오답: 2. 즉석 이해 불필요는 양쪽이 공유하지 않음, 3. 손 움직임은 A에만 해당(B는 비판적), 4. 화자에 안심감을 주는 측면은 핵심 공통점 아님.

opts: 企業や社会が望む人材と、教育学で目標とする人材は異なるから。

子ども一人一人に併せて、教育の内容を変えられないから。

questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: 子どもは、教えたとおりに育つとはかぎらないから. It means "Children do not necessarily grow up exactly as they are taught." Question Translation As a reason for considering that it is not a particularly significant issue, which one does the author state? Explanation The passage discusses how education's role in shaping society and individuals is limited due to its inherent uncertainties. The statement "決して大きいものではない" ("not significant") refers to the limited impact education might have because children do not always develop exactly according to the teachings they receive. This matches the description provided in answer 4, where children might not grow exactly as taught, which outlines the inherent unpredictability and probability-linked nature of education's influence stated in the passage. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 企業や社会が望む人材と、教育学で目標とする人材は異なるから. ("Because the humans resources desired by companies and society are different from those targeted by educational objectives.") This is incorrect as the text does not focus on the mismatch between societal/enterprise expectations and educational goals, but rather on the nature of education's unpredictability and societal demands. - Answer 2: 「生きる力」をつけるために必要なことは、明確ではないから. ("Because what is necessary for developing 'living strength' is not clear.") The passage acknowledges "生きる力" as a parental desire but does not delve into its ambiguity as a main point for being insignificant. - Answer 3: 子ども一人一人に併せて、教育の内容を変えられないから. (
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (아이는 가르친 대로 자란다고는 할 수 없기 때문). 본문은 교육의 불확정성을 '교육학의 기본 중의 기본'으로 들어, 가르침과 성장이 비례하지 않는다고 설명. 오답: 1. 인재 차이 논의 아님, 2. '生きる力'의 모호함이 주된 이유 아님, 3. 개별 대응 불가 언급 없음.

questions.stem: 教育について、筆者の考えに合うのはどれか。
opts: それぞれの目的に合わせて合理化・効率化しなければ、機能しない。
みんなで一つの目的を共有しなければ、効果は上がらない。
特定の目的を果たすために存在するものではない。
役に立つかどうかという観点で評価されるべきだ。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 3: 特定の目的を果たすために存在するものではない. It means that education does not exist solely for the purpose of fulfilling a specific objective. Question Translation Which choice aligns with the author's view on education? Explanation In the passage, the author emphasizes that education should not be constrained or narrowly defined by the specific objectives of different social demands, such as economic or community needs. The author argues against the idea of simplifying or optimizing education to serve a singular purpose, highlighting that education contributes probabilistically and broadly to society but is not meant to serve one isolated goal or expectation. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: それぞれの目的に合わせて合理化・効率化しなければ、機能しない. This suggests that education must be optimized to meet various purposes to function properly. The author criticizes this idea, arguing that this approach would lead to a skewed educational system. - Answer 2: みんなで一つの目的を共有しなければ、効果は上がらない. This implies that education would be more effective if everyone shares a single purpose. The author contends that education should not be limited to serving one unified objective. - Answer 4: 役に立つかどうかという観点で評価されるべきだ. This posits that education should be evaluated based on its utility. However, the author criticizes viewing education solely from the standpoint of utility, explaining that education plays a complex role in society that cannot be reduced to mere usefulness. questions.stem: 筆者が最も言いたいことは何か。
opts: 多様な社会の要求に応じられるように、教育のあり方を考え直すべきだ。
社会の不安を取り除くためには、教育を合理化・効率化すべきではない。
教育の改革によって、安定した社会を持続させていくことが必要だろう。
現代社会の問題のすべてが、教育で解決できると思ってはいけない。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4: 現代社会の問題のすべてが、教育で解決できると思ってはいけない。 It means "We must not think that all of the current societal problems can be solved through education." Question Translation What is the main thing the author wants to say? Explanation The passage discusses the limitations of education in solving societal problems. The author acknowledges the contributions of education but emphasizes its unpredictable nature and inability to solely address all societal issues. The passage points out the risk of placing excessive expectations on education, leading to an unbalanced and ineffective system. Therefore, Answer 4 best captures the author's main point — that societal issues are too complex to be entirely resolved through education alone. Why Other Options Are Incorrect - Answer 1: 多様な社会の要求に応じられるように、教育のあり方を考え直すべきだ。 ("We should reconsider the form of education to meet diverse social demands.") The passage does not suggest modifying education to meet various demands but rather warns against expecting education to solve all problems. - Answer 2: 社会の不安を取り除くためには、教育を合理化・効率化すべきではない。 ("To eliminate social anxiety, education should not be rationalized or made efficient.") This answer slightly touches on the theme but misplaces emphasis, as the passage highlights the dangers of overburdening education, not just about not rationalizing it. - Answer 3: 教育の改革によって、安定した社会を持続させていくことが必要だろう。 ("It is necessary to sustain a stable society through educational reform.") Although education reform is m
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (현대 사회의 문제 모두를 교육으로 해결할 수 있다고 생각해서는 안 된다). 본문 핵심은 교육에 과도한 기대를 거는 것에 대한 경종이며, 교육의 한계를 인식해야 한다는 주장. 오답: 1. 교육 형태 재검토는 주된 메시지 아님, 2. 합리화 거부는 부분적, 3. '교육 개혁으로 사회 지속'은 본문 비판 대상.

questions.stem: 次の 4 人は今年の４月に、運動教室を受講したいと考えている。この中で、この教室を受講できる人は誰か。 名前 年齢 住んでいる所 勤務地 健康度測定 ダニエルさん 54歳 木原市内 木原市外 去年の1月に受けた 木村さん 34歳 木原市外 木原市内 去年の10月に受けた 大川さん 16歳 木原市内 なし 今年の2月に受けた チェさん 27歳 木原市内 木原市内 今年の5月に受ける予定
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 2 (木村さん / Mr. Kimura) is correct. He meets both eligibility conditions. Question Translation "Four people want to take the exercise class this April. Who among them can take it?" Explanation Eligibility requires both: ① living in the city or working in the city, and being 18 or older; ② having taken the center’s health fitness measurement within the past 1 year counting back from the class date. Mr. Kimura works in the city and took the measurement last October, which is within one year of April. He is also over 18. Why Other Options Are Incorrect 1. ダニエルさん — "Mr. Daniel" → He lives in the city (OK) and is 54 (OK), but his measurement was last January, which is over 1 year before April → 3. 大川さん — "Mr./Ms. Ōkawa" → Measurement this February (within 1 year) and lives in the city (OK), but 16 years old → under 18 → 4. チェさん — "Mr./Ms. Che" → 27 and lives/works in the city (OK), but the measurement is scheduled for May (future), so as of April they have not taken it within the past year → Grammar/Vocabulary Note 「受講日から過去1年以内に受けた」 = "taken within the past one year counting back from the class date" (must already be completed by the class day).
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (기무라 씨). 자격 조건은 ①시내 거주 또는 시내 근무·18세 이상, ②수강일 기준 과거 1년 이내 건강도 측정 수강. 기무라 씨는 시내 근무·34세, 작년 10월 측정(1년 이내)이라 모두 충족. 오답: 1. 다니엘 씨는 측정이 작년 1월(1년 초과), 3. 오카와 씨는 16세(18세 미만), 4. 체 씨는 측정이 5월 예정(수강일 4월 시점에 미수강).

questions.stem: シャヒンさんは、来週の「ダンス A」を予約したが、空きがあれば来週の「体操」も追加予約した いと考えている。どうすればいいか。
opts: 来週の月曜日以降に窓口か電話で空きを確認し、空きがあれば、窓口で予約する。
来週の月曜日以降に窓口か電話で空きを確認し、空きがあれば、予約機か電話で予約する。
来週の水曜日以降に窓口か電話で空きを確認し、空きがあれば、窓口で予約する。
来週の水曜日以降に窓口か電話で空きを確認し、空きがあれば、予約機か電話で予約する。
questions.expl: Correct Answer Answer 4 is correct. Question Translation "Shahin has booked next week’s ‘Dance A’ and wants to also add next week’s ‘Taisō (Exercise)’ if there’s a vacancy. What should they do?" Explanation An additional reservation is allowed only for classes that have not filled up and only starting two days before the class. ‘Taisō’ is on Friday, so from Wednesday (two days before) Shahin should check vacancies at the counter or by phone, and if there’s space, make the reservation by reservation machine or phone (since it’s the second and subsequent reservation, not the very first-ever one). Why Other Options Are Incorrect 1. 来週の月曜日以降に…窓口で予約する — "From next Monday… reserve at the counter" → Too early (Monday is 4 days before Friday; additional bookings start 2 days before). Also method is off for repeat reservations (should use machine/phone). 2. 来週の月曜日以降に…予約機か電話で予約する — "From next Monday… reserve by machine/phone" → Method is fine, but timing is wrong (must wait until Wednesday, two days before). 3. 来週の水曜日以降に…窓口で予約する — "From next Wednesday… reserve at the counter" → Timing is correct, but for second or later reservations the instructions say to use the reservation machine or phone, not the counter. Grammar/Vocabulary Note 「実施日の2日前に定員に達していないクラスに限り、…追加予約ができます」 clearly fixes the start timing for additional reservations and restricts them to classes with openings.
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (다음 주 수요일 이후 창구나 전화로 빈자리 확인, 빈자리 있으면 예약기 또는 전화로 예약). 추가 예약은 실시일 2일 전부터 정원 미달인 클래스에 한해 가능. 체조는 금요일이므로 수요일부터 확인 가능. 또한 두 번째 이후 예약은 예약기·전화로 진행. 오답: 1·2번은 월요일은 너무 이르고, 1·3번은 두 번째 이후 예약 방법(예약기·전화)을 어김.
